title: Berry Curvature Two-Form
summary: The Berry curvature two-form F is the gauge-invariant curvature of the Berry connection and integrates to the first Chern number on closed surfaces.
assumptions: The relevant band bundle is smooth on the domain.
regime: Regular momentum-space region or parameter-space surface.
scope: Lecture One curvature two-form of the occupied-state line bundle.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One identifies F/2pi with the first Chern class and notes dF=0 away from singularities.

representation: Gauge-invariant curvature two-form associated with Berry transport.
failure_modes: At singular points the form is not globally regular.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks for the geometric meaning of Berry curvature.
